aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description_wiki: Camp Grant may refer to:

description: county seat of Winnebago County, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Rockford is a city in Winnebago and Ogle counties in the U.S. state of Illinois.

description: city in and county seat of Salt Lake County, Utah, United States and the capital of Utah
description_wiki: Salt Lake City, often shortened to Salt Lake or SLC, is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Utah.

description: U.S. Army Medical Corps
consolidated city-county and capital of Colorado, United States
description_wiki: Denver  (  DEN-vər) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Colorado.

description: 1935 bomber aircraft family by Boeing
description_wiki: The Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress is an American four-engined heavy bomber aircraft that was developed in the mid-1930s for the United States Army Air Corps (USAAC).

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

surface_forms: 28th division was a National Guard outfit out of Pennsylvania
description: active United States Army National Guard formation
description_wiki: The 28th Infantry Division ("Keystone") is a unit of the United States Army National Guard, and is the oldest division-sized unit in the Army.

description: 1944 military action that took place during World War II
description_wiki: The Liberation of Paris (French: libération de Paris) was a battle that took place during World War II from 19 August 1944 until the German garrison surrendered the French capital on 25 August 1944.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: country in Western Europe
description_wiki: Luxembourg,  officially the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, is a landlocked country in Western Europe.

description: country in western Europe
description_wiki: Belgium, officially the Kingdom of Belgium, is a country in Northwestern Europe.

surface_forms: SS from the regular German Army
SS trooper was going to shoot him
description: Nazi paramilitary organization (1925–45) with the Waffen-SS as a military branch; conducted the mass murder of the European Jews (holocaust)
description_wiki:
The Schutzstaffel (German: [ˈʃʊtsˌʃtafl̩] ; lit. 'Protection Squadron'; SS; also stylised with SS runes as ᛋᛋ) was a major paramilitary organisation under Adolf Hitler and the Nazi Party in Nazi Germany, and later throughout German-occupied Europe during World War II.
It began with a small guard unit known as the Saal-Schutz ("Hall Security") made up of party volunteers to provide security for party meetings in Munich.

surface_forms: German prisoners dug into the ground
description: unified armed forces of Nazi Germany from 1935 to 1945
description_wiki: The Wehrmacht (German pronunciation: [ˈveːɐ̯maxt] , lit. 'defence force') were the unified armed forces of Nazi Germany from 1935 to 1945.

description: the standards of international law for humanitarian treatment in war
description_wiki: The Geneva Conventions are a series of four international treaties (1949) and their three additional protocols that form the core of international humanitarian law.

description: 20th-century international organisation, predecessor to the United Nations
description_wiki: The League of Nations (LN or LoN; French: Société des Nations [sɔsjete de nɑsjɔ̃], SdN) was the first worldwide intergovernmental organisation whose principal mission was to maintain world peace.

surface_forms: the Bulge was a real mess
the Bulge was the biggest loss of personnel
description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: private university in Chicago, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Roosevelt University is a private university in Chicago, Illinois, United States.

aliases: Act to provide Federal Government aid for the readjustment in civilian life of returning World War II veterans
description: United States law that provided a range of benefits for returning World War II veterans
description_wiki: A G.I. Bill is a colloquial name given to several pieces of legislation that have provided a range of benefits for American military veterans, particularly education benefits.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: retired British ocean liner
description_wiki: RMS Queen Mary is a historic retired British ocean liner that operated primarily on the North Atlantic Ocean from 1936 to 1967 for the Cunard Line.

description: harbour in the New York City area, USA
description_wiki: New York Harbor is a bay that covers all of the Upper Bay.

description: British overseas territory in the North Atlantic Ocean
description_wiki: Bermuda is a British Overseas Territory in the North Atlantic Ocean.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: Scotland is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

aliases: The Statue of Liberty Enlightening the World
description: American icon in New York, United States
description_wiki: The Statue of Liberty (Liberty Enlightening the World; French: La Liberté éclairant le monde) is a colossal neoclassical sculpture of a robed and crowned woman on Liberty Island in New York Harbor, within New York City.

description: combat operations and doctrine designed to nullify or reduce the effectiveness of hostile air action
description_wiki: Anti-aircraft warfare (AAW) or air defence (or air defense in American English) is the counter to aerial warfare and includes "all measures designed to nullify or reduce the effectiveness of hostile air action".

description: shop at a military post where goods and services are available to military personnel and authorized civilians
official region of England
description_wiki: The East Midlands is one of nine official regions of England.

description: 1944 landing operations of the Allied invasion of Normandy
description_wiki: The Normandy landings were the landing operations and associated airborne operations on 6 June 1944 of the Allied invasion of Normandy in Operation Overlord during the Second World War.

description: formation of the United States Army (1918/19, 1932-74 and since 1982)
city in Wallonia, Belgium
description_wiki: Bastogne (French pronunciation: [bastɔɲ] ; Dutch: Bastenaken [ˈbɑstəˌnaːkə(n)] ; German: Bastnach/Bastenach [ˈbast(ə)nax]; Luxembourgish: Baaschtnech [ˈbaːʃtnəɕ] ) is a city and municipality of Wallonia located in the province of Luxembourg in the Ardennes, Belgium.
